description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. Manage or coordinate fitness and wellness programs and services. Manage and train staff of wellness specialists, health educators, or fitness instructors. (Based on 20 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Probate wills and represent and advise executors and administrators of estates.

Evaluate findings and develop strategies and arguments in preparation for presentation of cases; analyze the probable outcomes of cases, using knowledge of legal precedents; examine legal data to determine advisability of defending or prosecuting lawsuits.

Help develop federal and state programs, draft and interpret laws and legislation, and establish enforcement procedures; interpret laws, rulings and regulations for individuals and businesses; advise clients concerning business transactions, claim liability, advisability of prosecuting or defending lawsuits, or legal rights and obligations.

Act as agent, trustee, guardian, or executor for businesses or individuals; negotiate settlements of civil disputes; represent clients in court or before government agencies; prepare, draft, and review legal documents, such as wills, deeds, patent applications, mortgages, leases, and contracts.

Perform administrative and management functions related to the practice of law; supervise legal assistants; confer with colleagues with specialties in appropriate areas of legal issue to establish and verify bases for legal proceedings; keep abreast of changes in the law and inform judges when cases are affected by such changes; review complaints, petitions, motions, or pleadings that have been filed to determine issues involved or basis for relief.

Gather evidence to formulate defense or to initiate legal actions by such means as interviewing clients and witnesses to ascertain the facts of a case; prepare legal briefs and opinions, and file appeals in state and federal courts of appeal; present evidence to defend clients or prosecute defendants in criminal or civil litigation; prepare briefs, legal memoranda, or statements of issues involved in cases, including appropriate suggestions or recommendations.

Study Constitution, statutes, decisions, regulations, and ordinances of quasi-judicial bodies to determine ramifications for cases; research laws, court decisions, documents, opinions, briefs, or other information related to cases before the court; investigate facts and law of cases and search pertinent sources, such as public records and internet sources, to determine causes of action and to prepare cases.

Prepare for trial by performing tasks such as organizing exhibits; prepare affidavits or other documents, such as legal correspondence, and organize and maintain documents in paper or electronic filing systems; conduct personal background investigations, such as pre-employment checks, to obtain information about an individual's character, financial status, or personal history; meet with clients and other professionals to discuss details of cases.

Negotiate contractual agreements; conduct private investigations on a paid basis; testify at hearings or court trials to present evidence; review dockets of pending litigation to ensure adequate progress; monitor regulatory affairs activities to ensure their alignment with corporate sustainability or green initiatives.

Keep and monitor legal volumes to ensure that the law library is up-to-date; prepare periodic reports on court proceedings, as required; maintain judges' law libraries by assembling or updating appropriate documents; assess fees related to registration of property-related documents; summarize pertinent legal or insurance details, or sections of statutes or case law from reference books for use in examinations or as proofs or ready reference.

Supervise or train surveillance observers; communicate with counsel regarding case management or procedural requirements; attend court sessions to hear oral arguments or record necessary case information; verify accuracy and completeness of land-related documents accepted for registration, preparing rejection notices when documents are not acceptable; conduct environmental audits to ensure adherence to environmental standards.

Prepare reports describing any title encumbrances encountered during searching activities and outlining actions needed to clear titles; prepare lists of all legal instruments applying to a specific piece of land and the buildings on it; examine individual titles to determine if restrictions, such as delinquent taxes, will affect titles and limit property use; examine documentation such as mortgages, liens, judgments, easements, plat books, maps, contracts, and agreements to verify factors such as properties' legal descriptions, ownership, or restrictions.

Review materials such as marketing literature or user manuals to ensure that regulatory agency requirements are met; implement or monitor complaint processing systems to ensure effective and timely resolution of all complaint investigations; investigate product complaints and prepare documentation and submissions to appropriate regulatory agencies as necessary; manage activities such as audits, regulatory agency inspections, or product recalls.

Develop risk management strategies based on assessment of product, compliance, or operational risks; direct activities of workers who search records and examine titles, assigning, scheduling, and evaluating work, and providing technical guidance as necessary; monitor operations to ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards.
Augment knowledge by participating in continuing education classes to maintain current knowledge of the industry; maintain court docket; assess fees; develop and maintain log of observations.

Coordinate judges' meeting and appointment schedules; screen, control, and handle evidence and exhibits during court proceedings; direct the preparation and submission of regulatory agency applications, reports, or correspondence; prepare and issue title commitments and title insurance policies, based on information compiled from title searches; monitor regulatory affairs trends related to relevant issues.

Provide assistance to the public, such as directions to court offices; examine legal data, tenant applications, or university requirements to provide accurate information; escort prisoners; report all violations and suspicious behaviors to supervisors, verbally or in writing; observe microanalysis of legal processes and subsequent patterns.

Organize and oversee fitness or wellness programs, such as training in first aid or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), along with fitness classes to improve strength, flexibility, cardiovascular conditioning, or general fitness.

Plan, organize, or lead group activities for customers, such as exercise routines, athletic events, or arts and crafts, and manage or oversee fitness or recreation facilities to ensure safety and cleanliness.

Develop or coordinate fitness and wellness programs or services, and provide individual support or counseling in general wellness or nutrition, while also tracking attendance and performance data related to wellness events.
Train staff in the use or sale of products, programs, and activities, and deliver training sessions for wellness and fitness staff, ensuring consistent standards in operations and service delivery.

Direct documentation efforts to ensure compliance with applicable regulations and standards, discuss emerging compliance issues with management, and verify that all regulatory policies and procedures are documented and implemented.

Collaborate with human resources departments to implement consistent disciplinary action strategies in cases of compliance standard violations, and advise internal management on compliance program operations.
Provide recommendations and solutions in crisis situations such as workplace violence, protests, and demonstrations, and monitor compliance systems to ensure their effectiveness.
Plan or direct spa services and programs and prepare or implement budgets for recreational activities or wellness programs.
Prepare reports or presentations to communicate wellness program results and coordinate health screenings or other preventive measures, such as blood pressure or cholesterol screenings.
Maintain wellness- and fitness-related records or reports and ensure compliance with internal policies and regulations, keeping informed about industry trends and best practices.
Manage or supervise staff, hiring or assigning tasks and working hours, and assess employee performance to improve work quality.
Discuss and provide customer information about programs and activities, responding to inquiries regarding wellness initiatives.
Supervise fitness or wellness specialists and implement improvements in communication or monitoring of compliance standards.
Identify and apply for project funding, and develop marketing campaigns to promote healthy lifestyle participation in fitness programs.
Conduct needs assessments or surveys to determine interest or satisfaction with wellness and fitness programs, ensuring a diverse range of offerings.

Develop processes or procedures for operations, including transitioning from construction to commercial operations; prepare operational budgets; supervise employees or subcontractors to ensure quality of work or adherence to safety regulations; and oversee the maintenance of equipment or structures.
